accurate.why: All claims are supported by the bill text or accurate context (F=academic, M=vocational students; IHRA definition adopted May 26, 2016).

no_hallucinations.why: No fabricated claims; the F/M category descriptions and agency references are accurate context.

states_purpose.why: Clearly identifies the purpose of revoking/denying student visas for antisemitic conduct posing foreign policy consequences.

changes_to_existing_law.why: Notes the bill amends section 214 of the INA to add the new nonimmigrant status restriction.

exceptions_conditions.why: Captures the material condition that the Secretary must determine the conduct poses potentially serious adverse foreign policy consequences.

effective_dates.why: Notes the bill applies to conduct occurring on or after the date of enactment.

major_provisions_covered.why: Covers both the visa revocation/denial directive and the section 214 amendment, plus the definitions.

objective_tone.why: Summary is neutral and descriptive without editorializing.

concise.why: No padding or repetition; appropriately compact.

correct_entities.why: Correctly identifies the Secretary of State, INA, section 214, and the IHRA/Department of State.

correct_figures.why: The only date cited, May 26, 2016, matches the bill and the factual record.
